steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $14x^2 + 6$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Identify like terms
steps.content: The expression $9x^2 + 6 + 5x^2$ has two $x^2$ terms: $9x^2$ and $5x^2$. The constant 6 has no like term.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $14x^4 + 6$: Adds the coefficients correctly (14) but raises the exponent to 4 — adding terms does not change the exponent. ✗
  • Choice B — $14x^2 + 6$: $9x^2 + 5x^2 = 14x^2$; the constant 6 remains. ✓
  • Choice C — $20x^4$: Adds 6 to the coefficient (giving 20) and changes the exponent to 4 — both errors. ✗
  • Choice D — $20x^2$: Absorbs the constant 6 into the coefficient ($14+6=20$) instead of keeping it separate. ✗
That is the correct answer.
